meta.source: Technical Analysis - Dr. Kanu Jain (Ralph Nelson Elliott)

market_discounts_everything.name: Market Discounts Everything
market_discounts_everything.description: All fundamental information is reflected in price action
rules: Price movements incorporate all known information
Focus on price patterns rather than fundamentals alone
Historical data is the primary input

prices_move_in_trends.name: Prices Move in Trends
prices_move_in_trends.description: Price movements follow identifiable trends for extended periods
rules: Most technical strategies assume trending behavior
Trade in direction of prevailing trend
Trends persist until clear reversal signal

history_repeats.name: History Tends to Repeat
history_repeats.description: Market participants react consistently to similar stimuli over time
rules: Chart patterns recur because human psychology is consistent
Past pattern behavior informs future probability
Pattern recognition requires experience

primary_trend.name: Primary Trend
primary_trend.description: Major trend lasting from less than a year to several years
rules: Can be bullish or bearish
Defines the macro market direction
Position trading aligns with primary trend

secondary_trend.name: Secondary Trend
secondary_trend.description: Counter-trend movement within primary trend, lasting weeks to months
rules: Retraces 33-66% of primary trend
Often mistaken for trend reversal
Swing trading opportunities within primary trend

minor_trend.name: Minor Trend
minor_trend.description: Day-to-day price movements lasting a few days
rules: Noise within larger trends
Not reliable for long-term decisions
Relevant for scalping and day trading

bull_market_impulse.name: Bull Market Impulse (1-2-3-4-5)
rules: Wave 1: Initial move up from bottom
Wave 2: Retracement (never below wave 1 start)
Wave 3: Strongest wave, never shortest
Wave 4: Correction (never overlaps wave 1 territory)
Wave 5: Final push, often on declining volume
fibonacci: Wave 5 equals wave 1 or 61.8% of wave 1-3

bear_market_impulse.name: Bear Market Impulse (1-2-3-4-5)
rules: Wave 1: Initial decline from top
Wave 2: Bounce (never above wave 1 start)
Wave 3: Strongest down wave
Wave 4: Rally (never overlaps wave 1)
Wave 5: Final decline, capitulation

abc_correction.name: A-B-C Corrective Pattern
abc_correction.description: Three-wave counter-trend correction after five-wave impulse
rules: Wave A: First counter-trend move
Wave B: Retracement of wave A (often traps traders)
Wave C: Final corrective move, often equals wave A
Corrections follow impulses in both bull and bear markets
fibonacci: Wave B retraces 50-78.6% of wave A
Wave C equals wave A or extends to 161.8%

zigzag.name: Zigzag Correction
rules: Wave B does not retrace more than 61.8% of wave A
Wave C extends beyond end of wave A
Common in wave 2 and wave 4 positions

flat.name: Flat Correction
flat.description: Sideways correction where wave B retraces near wave A start
rules: Wave B retraces 90%+ of wave A
Wave C barely exceeds wave A end
Indicates strong underlying trend

wave_3_trading.name: Trade Wave 3
wave_3_trading.description: Enter at end of wave 2 for wave 3 extension
rules: Wave 3 is typically the longest and most profitable
Confirm wave 2 completion with reversal pattern
Use Fibonacci 61.8% retracement of wave 1 as entry zone
Stop below wave 2 low

wave_5_exhaustion.name: Fade Wave 5 Exhaustion
wave_5_exhaustion.description: Prepare for reversal after wave 5 completion
rules: Wave 5 often shows RSI/MACD divergence
Volume declines relative to wave 3
Look for reversal patterns at wave 5 terminus
Do not short wave 5 prematurely — wait for confirmation

limitations: Requires significant experience for accurate wave counting
Subjective — different analysts may count differently
Not suitable for newly listed securities with limited history
Quick pattern identification skill is essential
